name: Accessity
description_short: Community Development Financial Institution offering small business loans up to $250,000 in Southern California, with wraparound support for underserved entrepreneurs.
description_long:
Accessity (formerly known by a previous name, rebranded in recent years) is a Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) that has been serving Southern California entrepreneurs since 1994. Over three decades, the organization has provided $52 million in loans to over 5,000 clients while offering business education and resources. The organization explicitly targets low-income entrepreneurs, entrepreneurs of color, women, and immigrant entrepreneurs who traditionally lack equitable access to capital and business support networks. Accessity was motivated to expand its impact after witnessing the disproportionate economic effects of the pandemic on underserved business communities.

Accessity's core offering is small business loans ranging up to $250,000, available for both startup ventures and business expansion. Beyond lending, the organization provides a comprehensive wraparound support system including connections to business and financial coaches, peer entrepreneur communities, free educational events and webinars, business resources, startup training programs, and an online marketplace to promote client businesses. Their approach emphasizes flexible lending designed for borrowers who may not qualify for traditional bank financing. The organization maintains partnerships with local and national resources and provides ongoing guidance before, during, and after the loan process.

What distinguishes Accessity is their explicit commitment to serving marginalized entrepreneur populations and their integrated community-based approach. They function as more than a lender—they position themselves as facilitators of an entrepreneurial ecosystem by connecting borrowers with peer networks, educational content, marketing channels through media partnerships, and business coaching. Their non-discrimination policy and focus on building social equity through lending philosophy reflects their values-driven mission. The organization's history as a CDFI and their emphasis on community strengthening rather than purely profit-driven lending sets them apart from traditional commercial lenders.

Accessity appears well-intentioned and mission-aligned, with documented impact over nearly 30 years. However, potential borrowers should note that specific loan terms, interest rates, approval requirements, and repayment timelines are not disclosed on the website. The organization serves only Southern California, which is a geographic limitation. While wraparound support is highlighted as a strength, the actual availability and depth of these services for each borrower is unclear from their public materials.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Loans up to $250,000 available for startups and business expansion
Established CDFI with 30+ years of track record ($52M+ in cumulative lending)
Explicit focus on underserved populations: entrepreneurs of color, women, immigrant entrepreneurs, and low-income business owners
Comprehensive wraparound support including business coaches, peer communities, free training programs, and educational events
Online marketplace to help promote and support Accessity-funded businesses
Free business resources, blog content, and webinars available to applicants and community
Strong local and national partner network for additional business support resources
cons: No interest rates, APR, fees, or specific loan terms disclosed on website
Geographic limitation: serves only Southern California region
No information about typical approval timeline or loan processing speed
Loan eligibility criteria and income/credit requirements not clearly specified
Unclear whether wraparound services (coaching, mentorship) are included with all loans or available selectively
best_for: Women entrepreneurs and entrepreneurs of color seeking capital for startup or expansion
Low-to-moderate income business owners unable to qualify for traditional bank loans
Immigrant entrepreneurs and minority-owned business owners in Southern California
Business owners who value community support and business education alongside capital access
diagnosis: Accessity is best suited for underserved entrepreneurs in Southern California who need capital up to $250,000 and value community connection and business education alongside financing. The main caveat is that critical lending terms—interest rates, fees, approval requirements, and repayment terms—are completely absent from their website, requiring direct contact to evaluate affordability and compare to alternatives.
services: Business loans up to $250,000 for startups and expansion
Business and financial coaching through partner network
Free business education blog and resources
Free events and webinars on business topics
Business resource partner connections and referrals
Online marketplace for marketing and promoting client businesses
Ongoing loan support before, during, and after funding
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
